data.title: U.S. Market News: U.S. Nonfarm Payrolls Rise by 178K in March; Unemployment Rate Falls to 4.3%
data.description: Key TakeawaysU.S. nonfarm payrolls increased by 178,000 in MarchJob growth came in below market expectationsUnemployment rate declined to 4.3%, beating forecastsLabor market signals remain mixed amid macro uncertaintyU.S. Job Growth Misses Expectations in MarchU.S. nonfarm payrolls rose by 178,000 in March, according to data reported on April 3. While the labor market continued to add jobs, the increase came in below market expectations, signaling a potential slowdown in hiring momentum.The latest figures highlight a cooling trend in employment growth, even as the broader labor market remains relatively resilient.Unemployment Rate Drops Below ForecastAt the same time, the U.S. unemployment rate declined to 4.3% in March, outperforming expectations of 4.4%. The drop suggests underlying strength in labor market participation despite softer payroll expansion.Mixed Signals for the U.S. EconomyThe combination of slower job growth and a lower unemployment rate presents mixed signals for the U.S. economy. While hiring appears to be moderating, the decline in unemployment indicates continued demand for labor.Markets are likely to interpret the data in the context of broader macro trends, including inflation pressures, interest rate expectations, and recent geopolitical developments.Market ImplicationsThe March labor report could influence expectations around Federal Reserve policy, particularly as investors assess whether the labor market is cooling enough to support potential rate adjustments.A softer payroll figure alongside stable unemployment may reinforce expectations of a gradual economic slowdown rather than a sharp contraction.

data.title: STOCKS | U.S. Bond Market Reacts to Labor Market Data Amid Holiday Closure
data.description: The New York Times financial market correspondent reports that while the stock market is closed for the Easter holiday, the bond market remains active, with trading continuing until noon local time. According to Jin10, investors initially interpreted the new data as an indication that the Federal Reserve can focus on reducing inflation, given the stable labor market. This likely suggests higher interest rates in the future. Following the data release, the yield on the two-year U.S. Treasury note, which is sensitive to changes in interest rate expectations, surged significantly to 3.85%.

data.title: The market seems to interpret the non-farm payrolls report as a sign of a stable labor market, suggesting the Federal Reserve will focus on reducing inflation.
data.description: A New York Times financial markets correspondent in New York reported that while the stock market was closed for the Easter holiday, the bond market remained open until noon local time. Investors initially seemed to interpret the new data as allowing the Federal Reserve to focus on reducing inflation, given the still-solid labor market. This likely implies higher interest rates. The yield on the two-year Treasury note, which is sensitive to changes in interest rate expectations, rose sharply to 3.85% after the data release. (Jinshi)

data.title: Federal Reserve Rate Cut Expectations for 2026 Diminish
data.description: Market pricing indicates a decline in expectations for Federal Reserve rate cuts in 2026. According to NS3.AI, this shift reflects changing investor sentiment regarding future monetary policy adjustments. The adjustment in market outlook suggests that investors are reassessing the likelihood of rate reductions by the Federal Reserve in the coming years.

data.title: Dollar Index Surges Post-Nonfarm Payroll Release
data.description: The dollar index (DXY) experienced a significant short-term increase, reaching a peak of 100.1 following the release of nonfarm payroll data. According to Jin10, non-U.S. currency pairs saw a broad decline, with the euro against the dollar (EUR/USD) and the British pound against the dollar (GBP/USD) both dropping nearly 30 points in a short span.

data.title: U.S. March Unemployment Rate Slightly Declines
data.description: The unemployment rate in the United States experienced a slight decline in March, reaching 4.3%, according to Jin10. This figure was lower than market expectations, which had anticipated the rate to remain steady at 4.4% compared to the previous month.

data.title: Following the release of the non-farm payrolls data, the US dollar index (DXY) surged briefly, reaching a high of 100.1.
data.description: Following the release of the non-farm payrolls data, the US dollar index (DXY) surged briefly, reaching a high of 100.1. Non-US dollar currencies generally fell, with the euro/dollar (EUR/USD) and the pound/dollar (GBP/USD) dropping nearly 30 points in the short term. (Jinshi)

data.title: U.S. March Nonfarm Payrolls Surpass Expectations with Significant Increase
data.description: The U.S. nonfarm payrolls for March showed a significant increase, according to Jin10. The seasonally adjusted nonfarm employment rose by 178,000, far exceeding the market expectation of 60,000. This marks the highest level since December 2024 and represents a substantial improvement from the negative growth observed in the previous month.

data.title: Cambodia's National Assembly Passes Cybercrime Bill Targeting Scam Networks
data.description:
Cambodia's National Assembly has unanimously approved a draft cybercrime law aimed at imposing severe penalties on leaders of large-scale scams. According to NS3.AI, the legislation proposes sentences ranging from 15 to 30 years, or even life imprisonment, for those found guilty. The bill is now set to be reviewed by the Senate and requires final approval from King Norodom Sihamoni.

This legislative move is part of Cambodia's broader effort to dismantle scam operations by April. The focus is on networks that exploit cryptocurrencies for cross-border fund transfers and money laundering activities.

data.title: Two people were sentenced to prison for illegally connecting to high-voltage power lines to mine Bitcoin in pigsties; the ringleader was sentenced to 10 years.
data.description: According to a report published on the China Judgments Online website, the Honggang District People's Court of Daqing City, Heilongjiang Province, recently released a first-instance judgment, sentencing Zhang to ten years in prison and Zhao to four years and ten months in prison for theft. Zhang and Zhao, for illegal profit, illegally tapped into high-voltage power lines at an oil field to steal electricity and set up 36 Bitcoin mining machines in an abandoned pigsty. The investigation revealed that on September 8, 2024, Zhang illegally tapped into a high-voltage power line at an oilfield owned by a limited liability company in Daqing, stealing electricity from an abandoned pigsty in a rented slaughterhouse to power his Bitcoin mining machines. Zhang purchased 24 mining machines. Later, Zhang contacted Zhao and invited him to join them in operating the Bitcoin mining machines. On December 12, 2024, Zhao, seeing Zhang profiting from cryptocurrency mining, knowingly participated in stealing electricity from an oil field to power Bitcoin mining rigs. Zhao funded the purchase of 12 mining rigs, and the two operated a total of 36 Bitcoin mining rigs. Zhang was responsible for contacting suppliers to purchase the rigs, illegally connecting to the power grid, providing operating space for the rigs, providing maintenance services, and selling Bitcoins. Zhao assisted Zhang with daily checks of the 36 machines and provided on-site maintenance for the rig switches. On August 1, 2025, Zhang and Zhao were arrested by the police. It was calculated that Zhang stole 565,375.2 kWh of electricity from the oil field, worth RMB 438,580.52. Zhao, from December 12, 2024 onwards, stole 468,060 kWh of electricity from the oil field, worth RMB 363,750.78. The court held that the defendants Zhang and Zhao, with the intent to illegally possess, stole public and private property, the amount of which was particularly large, and their actions constituted the crime of theft. This case was a joint crime; Zhang played a principal role and was the principal offender; Zhao played a secondary and auxiliary role and was an accessory. Given that both Zhang and Zhao confessed and pleaded guilty, and considering Zhao's status as an accessory, a lighter or reduced sentence was appropriate in accordance with the law. (China News Weekly)

data.title: Hyperliquid Expands Bug Bounty Program to Include Binary Market
data.description:
On April 3, Hyperliquid announced that the results of its binary market testnet transactions have been incorporated into the mainnet-level bug bounty program. According to BlockBeats, the current scope of the bug bounty does not include multi-result markets. While feedback on the front end is welcome, it is not eligible for rewards under the bug bounty program.

Previously, Hyperliquid revealed that HyperCore would support result trading (HIP-4). The result is a fully collateralized contract with a settlement price confined within a fixed range. This serves as a versatile foundational trading component, applicable to prediction markets and bounded options tools.

data.title: Hyperliquid plans to lower portfolio margin requirements following the next network upgrade.
data.description: Hyperliquid announced that following the next network upgrade, users with trading accounts valued at over $10,000 will be able to enable Portfolio Margin without any trading volume requirements. New users can immediately use this mode after depositing funds. Meanwhile, accounts with a weighted average trading volume of $5 million or more will continue to be eligible for Portfolio Margin, and this rule remains unchanged.

data.title: Cardano Foundation Reports Shift in Reserve Composition by End of 2025
data.description: The Cardano Foundation has reported a significant shift in its reserve composition by the end of 2025. According to NS3.AI, ADA accounted for 51.6% of the reserves, while Bitcoin's share increased to 25.5%, and cash and financial assets made up 22.9%. The foundation's total assets were valued at 287.5 million Swiss francs, marking a 45% decrease compared to the previous year. Additionally, the foundation allocated 23.6 million Swiss francs towards technology, adoption, and governance initiatives in 2025.

data.title: ALGO Surges Over 44% Following Google's Quantum Computing Research Mention
data.description: ALGO has experienced a significant increase of over 44% in the past week. According to NS3.AI, this surge follows Google's mention of Algorand in a March 31 quantum computing research paper, highlighting its role as an early adopter of quantum-resistant protocols. In the last 24 hours, ALGO added approximately 13% and briefly surpassed the $0.12 mark.

data.title: Figure Technology Solutions Reports Growth in YLDS Stablecoin Circulation
data.description: Figure Technology Solutions has published its operational report for the first quarter of 2026. According to NS3.AI, the report highlights that the circulation of the YLDS stablecoin has reached $598 million, marking a 2% increase from the previous month. Additionally, the trading volume on Figure's consumer loan marketplace approached $1.2 billion.

data.title: Bank of Canada: The cost of Aave V3's zero-non-performing loans is that all losses are transferred to the borrowers.
data.description: A working paper published by the Bank of Canada shows that Aave V3 achieved zero non-performing loans in 2024, but its risk control mechanism essentially transfers all losses to borrowers to protect lenders' interests (BOC). Based on transaction data from January 27, 2023 to May 6, 2025, the study found that Aave V3, relying on over-collateralization and automatic liquidation mechanisms, completed liquidation before the collateral value fell below the outstanding debt, effectively curbing lenders' losses. However, this model comes at the expense of borrowers' interests, and its capital efficiency is lower than traditional lending systems. The paper also points out that recursive leverage (i.e., repeatedly borrowing with collateral and then using the lent assets as new collateral in a cyclical operation) accounted for over 20% of the total loan volume and 8.2% of the number of loans, exacerbating borrowers' risk exposure during market downturns. Liquidation events showed a concentrated outbreak, with WETH, wstETH, WBTC, and weETH accounting for 90% of the total liquidation value. (Cointelegraph)

data.title: Binance Easter Egg Hunt Offers 8,000 USDC in Rewards
data.description:
According to the announcement from Binance, the platform is celebrating Easter with a community activity that offers participants the chance to share 8,000 USDC in token vouchers. The event is divided into two separate activities on Discord and WhatsApp, each with its own set of rules and rewards.

The Discord Egg Hunt runs from 2026-04-03 12:00 (UTC) to 2026-04-13 12:00 (UTC). Participants are required to join the Binance Discord server and search for a hidden Easter Egg emoji each day. Once found, users must note the number next to the emoji and submit it in the #easter-egg-hunt channel using the format “!egg” followed by the number. Daily rewards of 1,000 USDC will be distributed, with the first 10 users who correctly identify the daily egg number receiving $10 in USDC. Additionally, a final completion reward of 4,000 USDC will be shared among the first 1,000 users who submit all 10 egg numbers correctly, with each receiving 4 USDC. Participants can earn a maximum of 14 USDC during the activity period.

The WhatsApp Egg Hunt takes place from 2026-04-03 12:00 (UTC) to 2026-04-10 23:59 (UTC). Participants must join the Binance WhatsApp channel, where daily posts will provide hints to locate hidden egg numbers across the Binance ecosystem. Users are required to complete a daily survey with the correct egg number. A total of 3,000 USDC in token vouchers will be equally shared among users who answer all seven surveys correctly. Rewards will be distributed before 2026-04-30, and users can redeem their token voucher rewards via the Rewards Hub. Binance emphasizes that only verified users who confirm participation during the promotion period are eligible for rewards.

data.title: Diamante Financial Secures $1.5 Million for Quantum-Safe Blockchain Development
data.description: Diamante Financial, a developer of quantum-safe Layer-1 blockchain technology, has announced the completion of a $1.5 million strategic funding round. According to Odaily, the investment was led by Khofiz Shakhidi, founder of Jefferson Capital Ltd and co-founder of Minexx Ltd. The newly acquired funds will be utilized to build a production-grade Layer-1 encryption network based on post-quantum cryptography technology (FIPS 204) to address quantum security vulnerabilities faced by crypto assets.

data.title: Brickken Secures €3 Million Pre-A Funding to Enhance Tokenization Infrastructure
data.description: Brickken, a tokenization platform based in Barcelona, Spain, has announced the completion of a €3 million Pre-A funding round. According to Odaily, the investment was led by Dedagroup and GRX. The newly acquired funds will be utilized to strengthen the compliance of Brickken's tokenization infrastructure, which supports the tokenization of assets such as stocks, debt, bonds, funds, real estate, and commodities. Additionally, Brickken plans to expand its market presence in the European Union and the United Arab Emirates.

data.title: Figure releases Q1 operating data: YLDS stablecoin's circulating supply increased to $598 million.
data.description: Figure Technology Solutions, a Nasdaq-listed blockchain-native capital market company, released its Q1 2026 operating data report, disclosing that the circulating supply of its stablecoin YLDS has increased to $598 million, a 2% increase from the previous month. Furthermore, its platform's consumer lending market transaction volume is close to $1.2 billion. (Globenewswire)
